Assembly: The assembly csSphPalu1.1 is based on 30x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The genome is tetraploid so this assembly represents one copy of each chromosome from each sub genome. This includes a single copy of a putative sex chromosome and multiple copies of a michrochromosome which appears to be underrepresented based on read coverage.

Assembly: The assembly csSphGirg1.1 is based on 70x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. This assembly includes two chromosomes much smaller than the others which may be sex related although this could not be confirmed.

Assembly: The assembly sScyCan1.2 is based on 63x PacBio data, 43x 10X Genomics Chromium data, and BioNano data generated at the Wellcome Sanger Institute, and 17x Hi-C data generated at the Baylor College of Medicine. The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Falcon-unzip, retained haplotig identification with purge_dups, 10X based scaffolding with scaff10x, BioNano hybrid-scaffolding, Hi-C based scaffolding and improvement by the Aiden lab as part of a collaboration with DNA Zoo using 3D-DNA and Juicebox, Arrow polishing, and two rounds of FreeBayes polishing. Finally, the assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Assembly: The assembly csSphTere1.1 is based on 63x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial and chloroplast genomes were assembled using OATK. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome 21 and 22 are microchromosomes. Chromosome 20 is about 1/4th of the size of an autosome, which is similar to what Healey, A.L., et. al (https://doi.org/10.1038/s41477-022-01333-5) observed for potential U/V sex chromosomes in other Sphagnums.
